page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 72 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 576.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 112 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional performance in data-intensive operations. The server achieves top-tier ratings in multi-core Geekbench testing, software compilation, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier performance in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read and write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations, which reached 148.38 million operations per second, also rank in the top decile. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations sit in the average to strong ranges, while LLM text generation performance varies from poor on very small models to strong on larger 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i8ge.18xlarge leverages the ARM64 architecture to deliver high resource density, combining substantial memory capacity with massive local NVMe storage without the overhead of x86 licensing. However, tradeoffs include the lack of hardware-accelerated GPUs and the absence of x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX-512 or AMX, which may limit its suitability for certain legacy vector-heavy workloads or specialized AI training.

This instance is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput key-value stores like Redis, big data analytics, and intensive software compilation pipelines. Its massive local storage and high network bandwidth also make it appropriate for distributed file systems and high-volume static web serving.
description: The Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server powered by a 72-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 576.0 GB of RAM and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 112 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in database operations, Redis throughput, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. While it lacks GPU acceleration and x86 instruction sets like AVX-512, its high resource density offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput key-value stores, software compilation, and big data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server featuring 72 Graviton cores, 576 GB RAM, 45 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server with 72 Graviton cores, 576 GB RAM, and 45 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform delivering forty-five terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity and top-tier database performance for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 72 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
45,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
576.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read/write bandwidth
112 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis throughput
Optimized for large-scale databases, key-value stores, and big data analytics
